title: Legal-review signoff record (Phase 4.5b)
description: Machine-readable sign-off entry recording that qualified legal counsel reviewed a compliance-claim pull request. See docs/legal-review-guide.md for the full process.

$schema.description: Optional IDE-tooling hint pointing to this schema.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last time this file was updated (informational).

baseline_commit.description: Short or long SHA of the commit that introduced this file. Legal-review gating applies to commits AFTER this baseline; earlier tier-1 content is grandfathered (see docs/legal-review-guide.md §7).

signoffs.description: One record per PR (or direct commit) that passed the legal-review gate. Append-only — never rewrite history.

pr.description: GitHub PR number with leading '#', or the literal string 'direct-commit'.

commit.description: Short or long SHA of the commit counsel reviewed (the final reviewed commit after any requested revisions).

reviewer.description: Counsel's name and firm/role. Full name is intentional — the signoff file is public and auditors need an attributable review chain. Reviewers may redact to firm + role if they prefer not to be named (see docs/legal-review-guide.md §5).

reviewerRole.description: internal-counsel: employed by the adopting organisation. external-counsel: outside law firm. paralegal: permitted for clause-number accuracy only; not for primer/evidence-pack interpretation (see guide §2.1).

regulations.description: Tier-1 regulations reviewed. Limited to the 12 tier-1 frameworks because those are the only ones that trigger legal review (see guide §1).

ucs.description: UC IDs whose compliance[] entries counsel reviewed. Empty when review was scoped to primer/evidence-pack prose only.

documents.description: Document paths (with optional #anchor) reviewed — typically docs/regulatory-primer.md, docs/evidence-packs/*.md, or data/regulations.json.

outcome.description: See docs/legal-review-guide.md §3 for the effect of each outcome on the PR.

revisionsRequested.description: REQUIRED when outcome is 'approved-with-revisions'. One string per revision counsel required.

caveats.description: REQUIRED when outcome is 'conditional'. Each caveat is propagated into a legalCaveat field on the relevant UC sidecar compliance[] entry.

notes.description: Free text. Typically records the regulation version/date counsel consulted, or explains a scope-downgrade rationale.
